failure_surface: Formulas written to cells K6:K10 contain literal placeholder strings '${target_item}' and '${lookup_date}' instead of actual cell values, making them invalid Excel formulas.
root_cause: Agent constructed Excel formulas using Python f-strings where variable substitution appeared correct, but the resulting formula strings still contained unexpanded template literals. The AGGREGATE-based lookup formula referenced non-existent placeholders instead of embedding actual cell references or values for the item name and date comparison.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When constructing dynamic Excel formulas, explicitly substitute all variable values from cells into the formula string before assignment, ensuring no template-like placeholders remain. Verify formula content after writing by checking for unexpected literal strings.
evidence: Verification output shows formulas contain literal '${target_item}' and '${lookup_date}' strings: 'K6: ...(($B$3:$B$13="${target_item}")...'
Code attempted f-string formatting with 'formula = f"=INDEX(...(\"{target_item}\")...)"' but result shows placeholder text persisted
Initial spreadsheet inspection showed existing formulas also had placeholder patterns suggesting agent copied incorrect template structure

memory_items.title: Dynamic Formula Construction Validation
memory_items.lesson: When building Excel formulas programmatically with variable cell references, verify all template placeholders are fully substituted before assignment.
memory_items.generalization: After constructing dynamic formulas in spreadsheets, always check for remaining curly-brace patterns (${var}, #{ref}) or escaped template markers that indicate incomplete substitution.

memory_items.title: Cell Value vs Cell Reference Distinction
memory_items.lesson: Distinguish between embedding actual cell VALUES versus CELL REFERENCES in formula construction; both approaches require different handling in openpyxl.
memory_items.generalization: For lookup formulas comparing against criteria, either use INDIRECT to reference cells dynamically, or extract values and embed them as string literals in the formula syntax.

memory_items.title: Formula Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Always verify formula content after writing, not just existence; partial string displays may mask missing substitutions.
memory_items.generalization: When validating written formulas, inspect full formula content for unexpected literal characters, placeholder text, or mismatched cell references before declaring success.

edits.content:
## Dynamic Formula Construction
- **Validate Substitution**: After constructing dynamic formulas with variables, verify no template placeholders (${var}, {ref}) remain in the final formula string.
- **Value vs Reference**: Distinguish between embedding actual cell VALUES versus CELL REFERENCES; formulas comparing criteria need explicit handling for both cases.
- **Full Content Check**: Always inspect complete formula content after writing—partial displays may hide missing substitutions or corrupted references.
